name: render_samples
description: Render N sample problems AND run the audit in one call. Returns `{samples: [...], audit: {ok, issues?}}`. Each sample has the EXACT rendered question, answer, and solution that students will see. The audit field reports any banlist hits or variable-leak issues. Use this as your single inspection tool — render + audit together. If audit.ok is true and the samples look right, go straight to save.

yaml.description: Full YAML spec (everything except difficulty, which the system injects)

n.description: Number of samples to render (1-5). Default 3.

name: audit
description: Run the mechanical banlist + variable-leak audit on your YAML. Returns ok=true if clean, otherwise a list of specific issues. Faster than render_samples and useful for a quick check after edits. Doesn't catch math errors — use render_samples and the CAS tools for that.

name: find_similar_yaml
description: Look up an existing well-formed YAML on the same or a similar topic and return it as a reference. Use this at the start to anchor your structure on something that already passes the audit. Returns the closest match by topic-path overlap.

name: compute
description: Evaluate any expression through SymEngine and return the canonical form. Use for ALL arithmetic — never compute (-10)^2 - 4*6*(-4) in your head, ask this tool. Returns the simplified expression as a string.

name: solve
description: Solve `expr = 0` for `var`. Returns roots as a comma-separated string. Linear and quadratic only. For finding x in `2*x + 3 = 7`, rearrange first: solve(`2*x + 3 - 7`, `x`).

name: differentiate
description: Symbolic derivative of `expr` with respect to `var`.

name: integrate
description: Symbolic antiderivative of `expr` with respect to `var`. If `lo` and `hi` are provided, returns the definite integral from `lo` to `hi`. Polynomial in `var` only.

name: evaluate_at
description: Substitute `var = val` in `expr` and reduce. Use for verifying that f(c) gives the value you claim, or for computing the answer at a point.

name: expand
description: Algebraically expand `expr`. Use to turn `(x - 3)*(x + 5)` into `x^2 + 2*x - 15`.

name: polynomial_from_roots
description: Given a list of roots (integers or rationals like '1/2'), return the expanded polynomial that has exactly those roots. Use to build a quadratic for `solve` or `factor` problems where you've decided the roots first.

name: matrix_with_eigenvalues
description: Given two integer eigenvalues, return a 2×2 integer matrix that has them as eigenvalues. Use this BEFORE building any system_of_odes / eigenvalue YAML — picking the matrix entries first and hoping the eigenvalues are clean is how the systems_of_odes problems went wrong.

name: solve_linear_inequality
description: Solve a linear inequality `a*x + b OP c*x + d` for x. Returns `{op: "<"|">"|"<="|">=", value: "<rhs>"}` with the inequality direction correctly flipped if you divide by a negative coefficient — the failure mode that breaks every linear-inequality YAML when done by hand. Pass the four coefficients and the original operator string.

a.description: Coefficient of x on left side

c.description: Coefficient of x on right side

name: quadratic_roots
description: Given a, b, c, return the discriminant, case, and roots for `a*x^2 + b*x + c = 0`. Returns `{discriminant, case: "real_distinct"|"real_repeated"|"complex", roots: ["<r1>", "<r2>"]}`. Use this BEFORE designing a quadratic problem so you know which case the YAML must handle.

name: verify_answer_key
description: Render one sample of the YAML and confirm that the question is self-consistent: the answer_key, when graded against itself, returns Correct, AND the solution's last step matches the answer. Use this after render_samples and before save when the YAML's answer derivation is non-trivial. Returns `{ok: bool, mismatches: [...]}`.

name: save
description: Finalize the YAML. Pass the complete final YAML. Returns ok=true if it passes all audits and is accepted; otherwise returns ok=false with the remaining issues. Only call when render_samples shows clean output and audit returns ok=true. Call exactly once when you're confident.
